zh.of Studley, Warwickshire, England: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾斯圖德利
zh.Studley, Warwickshire, England: 英國瓦維其可爾斯圖德利

zh.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley是William IV Corbucion的兒子，於大約1244年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾斯圖德利。他於1264年與Sirke Studley在英國瓦維其可爾斯圖德利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1320年之後在英國瓦維其可爾斯圖德利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley是William IV Corbucion的儿子，于大约1244年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾斯圖德利。他于1264年与Sirke Studley在英国瓦維其可爾斯圖德利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1320年之后在英国瓦維其可爾斯圖德利。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Studley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called with Old English stōd ‘stud, horses’ + lēah ‘clearing’, including Studley (Oxfordshire, Wiltshire, Warwickshire, Yorkshire), and especially Stoodleigh (Devon) and Stoodleigh in West Buckland (Devon).

variants.name: Stoodley
meanings: English (Somerset and Dorset): variant of   Studley  .

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley was born about 1244, in Studley, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of William IV Corbucion. He married Sirke Studley in 1264, in Studley, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died after 1320, in Studley, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley est né environ 1244, à Studley, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de William IV Corbucion. Il a épousé Sirke Studley en 1264, à Studley, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé après 1320, à Studley, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley wurde um 1244 in Studley, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von William IV Corbucion geboren. Er hat 1264 in Studley, Warwickshire, England Sirke Studley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1320 in Studley, Warwickshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley è nato il circa 1244 nel Studley, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di William IV Corbucion. Lui sposato Sirke Studley nel 1264, nel Studley, Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1320, nel Studley, Warwickshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studleyは約1244年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストゥドリーでWilliam IV Corbucionの息子として生まれた。1264年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストゥドリーでSirke Studleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1320年以後にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストゥドリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley(은)는 대략 1244년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스투들리에서 William IV Corbucion의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1264년 영국 워윅실르 스투들리에서 Sirke Studley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1320년 이후 영국 워윅실르 스투들리에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley nasceu aproximadamente 1244, em Studley, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de William IV Corbucion. Ele casou-se com Sirke Studley em 1264, em Studley, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu após 1320, em Studley, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley родился около 1244 г. в местности студли, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали William IV Corbucion. Он женился на женщине по имени Sirke Studley в 1264 г. в местности студли, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер после 1320 г. в местности студли, варвиккшир, Англия.

sm.Abt 1244–after 1320: e tusa o le 1244–ina ua ma'ea le 1320

es.lifeSummary: Peter Corbicon of Studley nació aproximadamente 1244, en Studley, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de William IV Corbucion. Se casó con Sirke Studley en 1264, en Studley, Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1320, en Studley, Warwickshire, Inglaterra.
